days.summary: Today’s AI conversation was led by one clearly urgent developer issue—a reported compromise of LiteLLM packages on PyPI—alongside two OpenAI governance/product signals and a notable multimodal video-generation research thread. The strongest topics combined immediate operational impact, credible primary-source evidence, and broader implications for how AI systems are built and governed.

Today’s strongest AI hotspots were unusually concentrated: a high-severity supply-chain security incident affecting LiteLLM, a notable OpenAI strategic update around its Foundation and resource allocation, and a mechanistic-interpretability thread around safety circuit attribution that drew both research and practitioner attention. A diffusion-theory paper also stood out as substantive, but it looked more important for specialists than as a day-defining ecosystem event.
